QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_19

question.text: On the National Stock Exchange (NSE), the contract cycle for equity index and stock futures consists of:

answer.text: Three monthly contracts - the near month, next month, and far month - with new contracts introduced on the day after the expiry of the near month contract
Four quarterly contracts expiring in March, June, September, and December of each year
Weekly contracts that expire every Friday, with four contracts available at any given time
Two monthly contracts - the current month and next month only

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** NSE follows a 3-month rolling contract cycle. At any point, three contracts are available: (1) Near month (current month), (2) Next month (month after current), and (3) Far month (third month). When the near month expires, the next month becomes the new near month, and a new far month contract is introduced.
**The Trap:** Quarterly contracts are used in some international markets and for currency futures in India, not for equity futures. Weekly contracts exist for options but not for equity futures. There are always THREE months available, not two.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Contract cycle is a fundamental specification tested in Series VIII. Also relevant for Series XIII (currency derivatives).
**Key Takeaway:** Equity futures on NSE = 3-month rolling cycle: Near, Next, Far months.
